159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass

178	def apply_lora_mlp_swiglu(self, X, inplace = True):
179	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.gate_proj)
180	    gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS = get_lora_parameters(self.gate_proj)
181	    upW,     upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS = get_lora_parameters(self.  up_proj)
182	    downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS = get_lora_parameters(self.down_proj)
183	    out = LoRA_MLP.apply(X,
184	                         gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
185	                         upW,     upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
186	                         downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
187	                         swiglu_fg_kernel, swiglu_DWf_DW_dfg_kernel,
188	                         inplace,)
189	    return out

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)
301	        d_KA = torch.empty_like(KA)
302	        d_KB = torch.empty_like(KB)
303	        d_VA = torch.empty_like(VA)
304	        d_VB = torch.empty_like(VB)
305	
306	        # Q Projection
307	        # d_QA = X.t() @ (dQ @ QB.t())
308	        # d_QB = (QA.t() @ X.t()) @ dQ
309	        # d_QA *= QS
310	        # d_QB *= QS
311	        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha = QS, beta = 0)
312	        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha = QS, beta = 0)
313	
314	        # K Projection
315	        # d_KA = X.t() @ (dK @ KB.t())
316	        # d_KB = (KA.t() @ X.t()) @ dK
317	        # d_KA *= KS
318	        # d_KB *= KS
319	        d_KA.addmm_(X.t(), dK @ KB.t(), alpha = KS, beta = 0)
320	        d_KB.addmm_(KA.t() @ X.t(), dK, alpha = KS, beta = 0)
321	
322	        # V Projection
323	        # d_VA = X.t() @ (dV @ VB.t())
324	        # d_VB = (VA.t() @ X.t()) @ dV
325	        # d_VA *= VS
326	        # d_VB *= VS
327	        d_VA.addmm_(X.t(), dV @ VB.t(), alpha = VS, beta = 0)
328	        d_VB.addmm_(VA.t() @ X.t(), dV, alpha = VS, beta = 0)
329	
330	        # Combine derivatives to find dX
331	        # dQ
332	        QW = fast_dequantize(QW.t(), QW_quant)
333	        dX = torch.matmul(dQ, QW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
334	        del QW
335	        # dX += (dQ @ QB.to(dtype).t() @ (QS * QA.to(dtype).t()))
336	        dX.addmm_(dQ @ QB.t(), QA.t(), alpha = QS)
337	
338	        # dK
339	        KW = fast_dequantize(KW.t(), KW_quant)
340	        # dX += dK @ KW.t()
341	        dX.addmm_(dK, KW.t())
342	        del KW
343	        # dX += dK @ KB.to(dtype).t() @ (KS * KA.to(dtype).t())
344	        dX.addmm_(dK @ KB.t(), KA.t(), alpha = KS)
345	
346	        # dV
347	        VW = fast_dequantize(VW.t(), VW_quant)
348	        # dX += dV @ VW.t()
349	        dX.addmm_(dV, VW.t())
350	        del VW
351	        # dX += dV @ VB.to(dtype).t() @ (VS * VA.to(dtype).t())
352	        dX.addmm_(dV @ VB.t(), VA.t(), alpha = VS)
353	
354	        # QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
355	        # KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
356	        # VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
357	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
358	            None, None, d_QA.t(), d_QB.t(), None, \
359	            None, None, d_KA.t(), d_KB.t(), None, \
360	            None, None, d_VA.t(), d_VB.t(), None, \
361	            None,
362	    pass
363	pass

381	class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
382	    """
383	    ### LoRA weights
384	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
385	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
386	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
387	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
388	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
389	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
390	
391	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
392	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
393	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
394	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
395	
396	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
397	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
398	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
399	
400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass
457	pass

463	    O = LoRA_W.apply(X, OW, OW_quant, OA, OB, OS)
464	    return O

469	@torch._disable_dynamo
470	def fast_lora_forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
471	    raise NotImplementedError(
472	        "Unsloth: Currently not supported yet - reshaping done incorrectly"
473	    )
474	    self._check_forward_args(x, *args, **kwargs)
475	    adapter_names = kwargs.pop("adapter_names", None)
476	
477	    if self.disable_adapters:
478	        if self.merged:
479	            self.unmerge()
480	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
481	    elif adapter_names is not None:
482	        result = self._mixed_batch_forward(x, *args, adapter_names=adapter_names, **kwargs)
483	    elif self.merged:
484	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
485	    else:
486	        # Fastpath
487	        if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
488	            active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
489	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys(): return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
490	
491	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
492	            if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
494	                lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
495	                scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
496	                W = self.base_layer.weight
497	                return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
498	            pass
499	        pass
500	
501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530	
531	                result = result + self.lora_magnitude_vector[active_adapter](
532	                    x,
533	                    lora_A=lora_A,
534	                    lora_B=lora_B,
535	                    scaling=scaling,
536	                    base_layer=self.get_base_layer(),
537	                    base_result=base_result,
538	                )
539	            if requires_conversion:
540	                result = result.to(expected_dtype)
541	
542	    return result

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass

2413	def unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged(
2414	    self,
2415	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
2416	    tokenizer            = None,
2417	    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
2418	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
2419	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
2420	    is_main_process      : bool = True,
2421	    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
2422	    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
2423	    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
2424	    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
2425	    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
2426	    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
2427	    tags                 : List[str] = None,
2428	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
2429	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
2430	):
2431	    """
2432	        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
2433	        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.
2434	
2435	        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
2436	        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
2437	        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
2438	        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
2439	    """
2440	    if tokenizer is None:
2441	        logger.warning_once(
2442	            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
2443	            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
2444	        )
2445	    pass
2446	
2447	    arguments = dict(locals())
2448	    arguments["model"] = self
2449	    del arguments["self"]
2450	    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
2451	    for _ in range(3):
2452	        gc.collect()
2453	pass

2572	    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

2595	    elif "tags" in arguments:

2609	
2610	    if "commit_description" in arguments:
2611	        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
2612	        if commit_description is not None:
2613	            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
2614	            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
2615	                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
2616	        else:
2617	            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
2618	        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description
2619	
2620	    # Update model tag

2628	
2629	    try:

2634	    pass
2635	
2636	    if hasattr(self, "config"):
2637	        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
2638	    pass
2639	    '''
2640	    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())
2641	
2642	    original_model = model

2644	
2645	        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
2646	            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
2647	            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)

2650	            pass

## UNSLOTH PYTHON PACKAGE CODEBASE EXPLORATION REPORT

### 1. OVERALL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

```
/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476238580-9yau5k/
├── unsloth/                          # Main package directory
│   ├── __init__.py                   # Package initialization & imports
│   ├── save.py                       # Core save/export functionality (105KB)
│   ├── chat_templates.py             # Chat template handling
│   ├── tokenizer_utils.py            # Tokenizer utilities
│   ├── trainer.py                    # Trainer patching
│   ├── import_fixes.py               # Import compatibility fixes
│   ├── models/                       # Model implementations
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── loader.py                 # FastLanguageModel, FastModel loaders
│   │   ├── llama.py                  # FastLlamaModel (Llama-specific)
│   │   ├── mistral.py                # FastMistralModel
│   │   ├── qwen2.py, qwen3.py        # Qwen models
│   │   ├── gemma.py, gemma2.py       # Gemma models
│   │   ├── granite.py                # Granite model
│   │   ├── falcon_h1.py              # Falcon model
│   │   ├── dpo.py                    # DPO trainer patches
│   │   ├── rl.py, rl_replacements.py # Reinforcement learning
│   │   ├── vision.py                 # Vision model support
│   │   ├── _utils.py                 # Model utilities
│   │   ├── mapper.py, loader_utils.py
│   ├── kernels/                      # CUDA kernels
│   │   ├── fast_lora.py              # LoRA-specific kernels
│   │   ├── utils.py                  # Kernel utilities
│   │   └── [other CUDA kernel files]
│   ├── registry/                     # Model registry
│   ├── dataprep/                     # Data preparation utilities
│   └── utils/
├── tests/
│   └── saving/
│       └── test_unsloth_save.py       # Save functionality tests
└── pyproject.toml                    # Project config
```

### 2. SAVE/EXPORT METHODS DEFINED

The main save/export functionality is in **/unsloth/save.py** with these key functions:

#### Main Save Entry Point:
- **`unsloth_save_model()`** (Line 205-760)
  - Signature: `unsloth_save_model(model, tokenizer, save_directory, save_method="lora", push_to_hub=False, ...)`
  - Supports three save methods: `"lora"`, `"merged_16bit"`, `"merged_4bit"`
  - Handles pushing to Hugging Face Hub
  - Key parameters:
    - `save_method`: Type of save ("lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit")
    - `push_to_hub`: Boolean for uploading to HF
    - `token`: HF authentication token
    - `max_shard_size`: Default "5GB"
    - `safe_serialization`: Use safetensors format

#### GGUF Export Methods:
- **`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()`** (Line 1768-1950)
  - Converts 4-bit weights to float16, then to GGUF format
  - Supports multiple quantization methods (q4_k_m, q8_0, q5_k_m, etc.)
  - Installs llama.cpp automatically
  - Parameters:
    - `quantization_method`: Type of quantization
    - `first_conversion`: f16, bf16, f32, or q8_0
    
- **`unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()`** (Line 1951-2000+)
  - GGUF variant that pushes to Hub

#### GGML Export Methods:
- **`unsloth_save_pretrained_ggml()`** (Line 2211-2275)
  - Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format
  
- **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()`** (Line 2143-2210)
  - GGML + Hub upload combo

#### Generic Save Methods (Model-Agnostic):
- **`unsloth_generic_save()`** (Line 2356-2410)
  - Generic save for any model type
  
- **`unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()`** (Line 2413-2454)
  - Method attached to model: `.save_pretrained_merged()`
  
- **`unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()`** (Line 2456-2499)
  - Method attached to model: `.push_to_hub_merged()`

#### TorchAO Quantization:
- **`unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()`** (Line 2502+)
  - TorchAO quantization support

#### GGUF Conversion (Generic):
- **`save_to_gguf_generic()`** (Line 2276-2355)
  - Generic GGUF conversion for vision/non-standard models

#### Model-Patching Function:
- **`patch_saving_functions()`** (Line 2569-2678)
  - Patches the model instance with save methods:
    - `.save_pretrained_merged()`
    - `.push_to_hub_merged()`
    - `.save_pretrained_gguf()`
    - `.push_to_hub_gguf()`
    - `.save_pretrained_ggml()`
    - `.push_to_hub_ggml()`
    - `.save_pretrained_torchao()`

### 3. LoRA ADAPTER WEIGHTS MANAGEMENT

#### LoRA Parameter Extraction:

**Location**: `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` Lines 190-257

- **`get_lora_parameters(proj)`** (Line 190-232)
  - Returns 5-tuple: `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)`
  - `W`: Base layer weight
  - `quant_state`: Quantization state (for bitsandbytes 4-bit)
  - `A`: LoRA adapter A matrix (from `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight`)
  - `B`: LoRA adapter B matrix (from `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight`)
  - `scaling`: LoRA scaling factor (from `proj.scaling[adapter]`)

- **`get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)`** (Line 235-257)
  - Same as above, but also extracts bias term
  - Returns 6-tuple: `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling, bias)`

#### LoRA Layer Classes:

**From PEFT library** (imported in save.py):
```python
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear as Peft_Linear
from bitsandbytes.nn import Linear4bit as Bnb_Linear4bit
```

These layers store:
- `lora_A[adapter_name]`: Linear layer containing A matrix weights
- `lora_B[adapter_name]`: Linear layer containing B matrix weights  
- `scaling[adapter_name]`: Scaling factor s (typically `lora_alpha / rank`)
- `active_adapters`: Which adapter is currently active
- `disabled_adapters`: Disabled adapters
- `merged`: Boolean indicating if LoRA is merged

#### LoRA Merging:

**Function**: `_merge_lora()` (Line 159-187)
- Merges LoRA weights into base weights: `W_merged = W + (A.T @ (s * B.T))`
- Handles 4-bit quantized models via `fast_dequantize()`
- Uses `torch.addmm_()` for efficient in-place matrix multiplication

#### LoRA in Forward Pass:

**File**: `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`

Key LoRA computation classes:
- **`LoRA_QKV`** (Line 224-363): Computes Q,K,V projections with LoRA
  - Saves A, B matrices for backward pass
  - Applies: `Q = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq * s`
  
- **`LoRA_MLP`** (Line 28-174): Computes gate/up/down projections with LoRA
  - Three sets of A,B matrices (gate, up, down)
  
- **`LoRA_W`** (Line 381-457): Generic single projection with LoRA

Functions extracting LoRA parameters in forward:
- `apply_lora_qkv()` (Line 366-378): Gets QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB
- `apply_lora_mlp_swiglu()` (Line 178-190): Gets gate/up/down LoRA params

### 4. MAIN MODEL CLASSES

#### Primary Model Classes:

**Location**: `/unsloth/models/`

1. **`FastLanguageModel`** (in loader.py, Line 107+)
   - Main entry point for loading LLMs
   - Methods:
     - `.from_pretrained(model_name, ...)`
     - `.get_peft_model(model, r=16, target_modules=[...], ...)`

2. **`FastModel`** (in loader.py, Line 526+)
   - Generic model class (extends FastLanguageModel)
   - Supports text and vision models
   
3. **`FastVisionModel`** (in loader.py, Line 995+)
   - Vision-specific models

4. **`FastLlamaModel`** (in llama.py, Line 1778+)
   - Llama-specific optimizations
   - Methods:
     - `.from_pretrained()` (Line 1815+)
     - `.get_peft_model()` (Line 2266+)

5. **Architecture-Specific Models**:
   - `FastMistralModel`, `FastQwen2Model`, `FastQwen3Model`, `FastGraniteModel`, `FastFalconH1Model`

#### Key Model Methods:

**`get_peft_model()` signature** (llama.py Line 2266+):
```python
def get_peft_model(
    model,
    r = 16,
    target_modules = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
    lora_alpha = 16,
    lora_dropout = 0.0,
    bias = "none",
    layers_to_transform = None,
    layers_pattern = None,
    use_gradient_checkpointing = "unsloth",
    random_state = 3407,
    use_rslora = False,
    modules_to_save = None,
    init_lora_weights = True,
    loftq_config = {},
    temporary_location = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    qat_scheme = None,
    **kwargs,
)
```

### 5. ONNX EXPORT CODE

**Status**: **NO ONNX EXPORT CODE FOUND**

Search results:
- Grep for "onnx", "ONNX", "export_to_onnx" in entire codebase: **0 matches**
- Only export formats supported are:
  - **GGUF** (llama.cpp format) 
  - **GGML** (LoRA GGML format)
  - **TorchAO** (PyTorch quantization)
  - **Hugging Face Hub** (Standard HF format with/without LoRA merging)

### 6. KEY ARCHITECTURAL INSIGHTS

#### Save Method Flow:

1. **LoRA-only save**:
   - Uses PEFT's default `save_pretrained()` 
   - Saves only A, B matrices and adapter config
   - Smallest file size (~5-10% of base model)

2. **Merged 16-bit save**:
   - Calls `_merge_lora()` on all LoRA layers
   - Dequantizes 4-bit to float32
   - Merges: `W_new = W_orig + A.T @ (s * B.T)`
   - Converts to float16
   - Compatible with llama.cpp/GGUF

3. **Merged 4-bit save**:
   - Uses `model.merge_and_unload()` from PEFT
   - Keeps quantization state
   - Useful for DPO/inference with lower VRAM

4. **GGUF export**:
   - Converts to merged 16-bit first
   - Installs llama.cpp
   - Converts HF format to GGUF via `convert-hf-to-gguf.py`
   - Quantizes via `llama-quantize` tool

#### Adapter Architecture:

- **Default adapter name**: `"default"`
- **Active adapters tracking**: `model.active_adapters` (list) or `model.active_adapter`
- **Multiple adapters**: Supported via PEFT (different adapters for different tasks)
- **Merging**: Can merge adapters back into base weights permanently

#### Weight Management:

- **Base layers**: `Bnb_Linear4bit` (bitsandbytes), `Peft_Linear4bit` (PEFT wrapped)
- **LoRA storage**: Separate `lora_A` and `lora_B` Linear layers
- **Quantization awareness**: Supports fake quantization (QAT) for training
- **Memory efficiency**: LoRA layers stay in low precision (usually int8 or fp16)

### 7. IMPORTANT FILE PATHS FOR REFERENCE

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/unsloth/save.py` | All save/export functionality (2679 lines) |
| `/unsloth/models/loader.py` | FastLanguageModel, FastModel classes |
| `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | Llama-specific optimizations & get_peft_model |
| `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` | LoRA forward/backward kernels |
| `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | get_lora_parameters, get_lora_parameters_bias |
| `/unsloth/__init__.py` | Package initialization & public API |
| `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` | Save functionality tests |

This codebase is highly optimized for LoRA fine-tuning with support for 4-bit quantization, GGUF export, and efficient memory management on GPUs.

### 2. SAVE/EXPORT METHODS DEFINED

The main save/export functionality is in **/unsloth/save.py** with these key functions:

#### Main Save Entry Point:
- **`unsloth_save_model()`** (Line 205-760)
  - Signature: `unsloth_save_model(model, tokenizer, save_directory, save_method="lora", push_to_hub=False, ...)`
  - Supports three save methods: `"lora"`, `"merged_16bit"`, `"merged_4bit"`
  - Handles pushing to Hugging Face Hub
  - Key parameters:
    - `save_method`: Type of save ("lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit")
    - `push_to_hub`: Boolean for uploading to HF
    - `token`: HF authentication token
    - `max_shard_size`: Default "5GB"
    - `safe_serialization`: Use safetensors format

#### GGUF Export Methods:
- **`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()`** (Line 1768-1950)
  - Converts 4-bit weights to float16, then to GGUF format
  - Supports multiple quantization methods (q4_k_m, q8_0, q5_k_m, etc.)
  - Installs llama.cpp automatically
  - Parameters:
    - `quantization_method`: Type of quantization
    - `first_conversion`: f16, bf16, f32, or q8_0
    
- **`unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()`** (Line 1951-2000+)
  - GGUF variant that pushes to Hub

#### GGML Export Methods:
- **`unsloth_save_pretrained_ggml()`** (Line 2211-2275)
  - Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format
  
- **`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()`** (Line 2143-2210)
  - GGML + Hub upload combo

#### Generic Save Methods (Model-Agnostic):
- **`unsloth_generic_save()`** (Line 2356-2410)
  - Generic save for any model type
  
- **`unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()`** (Line 2413-2454)
  - Method attached to model: `.save_pretrained_merged()`
  
- **`unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()`** (Line 2456-2499)
  - Method attached to model: `.push_to_hub_merged()`

#### TorchAO Quantization:
- **`unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()`** (Line 2502+)
  - TorchAO quantization support

#### GGUF Conversion (Generic):
- **`save_to_gguf_generic()`** (Line 2276-2355)
  - Generic GGUF conversion for vision/non-standard models

#### Model-Patching Function:
- **`patch_saving_functions()`** (Line 2569-2678)
  - Patches the model instance with save methods:
    - `.save_pretrained_merged()`
    - `.push_to_hub_merged()`
    - `.save_pretrained_gguf()`
    - `.push_to_hub_gguf()`
    - `.save_pretrained_ggml()`
    - `.push_to_hub_ggml()`
    - `.save_pretrained_torchao()`

### 3. LoRA ADAPTER WEIGHTS MANAGEMENT

#### LoRA Parameter Extraction:

**Location**: `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` Lines 190-257

- **`get_lora_parameters(proj)`** (Line 190-232)
  - Returns 5-tuple: `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling)`
  - `W`: Base layer weight
  - `quant_state`: Quantization state (for bitsandbytes 4-bit)
  - `A`: LoRA adapter A matrix (from `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight`)
  - `B`: LoRA adapter B matrix (from `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight`)
  - `scaling`: LoRA scaling factor (from `proj.scaling[adapter]`)

- **`get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)`** (Line 235-257)
  - Same as above, but also extracts bias term
  - Returns 6-tuple: `(W, quant_state, A, B, scaling, bias)`

#### LoRA Layer Classes:

**From PEFT library** (imported in save.py):
```python
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear as Peft_Linear
from bitsandbytes.nn import Linear4bit as Bnb_Linear4bit
```

These layers store:
- `lora_A[adapter_name]`: Linear layer containing A matrix weights
- `lora_B[adapter_name]`: Linear layer containing B matrix weights  
- `scaling[adapter_name]`: Scaling factor s (typically `lora_alpha / rank`)
- `active_adapters`: Which adapter is currently active
- `disabled_adapters`: Disabled adapters
- `merged`: Boolean indicating if LoRA is merged

#### LoRA Merging:

**Function**: `_merge_lora()` (Line 159-187)
- Merges LoRA weights into base weights: `W_merged = W + (A.T @ (s * B.T))`
- Handles 4-bit quantized models via `fast_dequantize()`
- Uses `torch.addmm_()` for efficient in-place matrix multiplication

#### LoRA in Forward Pass:

**File**: `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`

Key LoRA computation classes:
- **`LoRA_QKV`** (Line 224-363): Computes Q,K,V projections with LoRA
  - Saves A, B matrices for backward pass
  - Applies: `Q = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq * s`
  
- **`LoRA_MLP`** (Line 28-174): Computes gate/up/down projections with LoRA
  - Three sets of A,B matrices (gate, up, down)
  
- **`LoRA_W`** (Line 381-457): Generic single projection with LoRA

Functions extracting LoRA parameters in forward:
- `apply_lora_qkv()` (Line 366-378): Gets QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB
- `apply_lora_mlp_swiglu()` (Line 178-190): Gets gate/up/down LoRA params

### 4. MAIN MODEL CLASSES

#### Primary Model Classes:

**Location**: `/unsloth/models/`

1. **`FastLanguageModel`** (in loader.py, Line 107+)
   - Main entry point for loading LLMs
   - Methods:
     - `.from_pretrained(model_name, ...)`
     - `.get_peft_model(model, r=16, target_modules=[...], ...)`

2. **`FastModel`** (in loader.py, Line 526+)
   - Generic model class (extends FastLanguageModel)
   - Supports text and vision models
   
3. **`FastVisionModel`** (in loader.py, Line 995+)
   - Vision-specific models

4. **`FastLlamaModel`** (in llama.py, Line 1778+)
   - Llama-specific optimizations
   - Methods:
     - `.from_pretrained()` (Line 1815+)
     - `.get_peft_model()` (Line 2266+)

5. **Architecture-Specific Models**:
   - `FastMistralModel`, `FastQwen2Model`, `FastQwen3Model`, `FastGraniteModel`, `FastFalconH1Model`

#### Key Model Methods:

**`get_peft_model()` signature** (llama.py Line 2266+):
```python
def get_peft_model(
    model,
    r = 16,
    target_modules = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],
    lora_alpha = 16,
    lora_dropout = 0.0,
    bias = "none",
    layers_to_transform = None,
    layers_pattern = None,
    use_gradient_checkpointing = "unsloth",
    random_state = 3407,
    use_rslora = False,
    modules_to_save = None,
    init_lora_weights = True,
    loftq_config = {},
    temporary_location = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    qat_scheme = None,
    **kwargs,
)
```

### 5. ONNX EXPORT CODE

**Status**: **NO ONNX EXPORT CODE FOUND**

Search results:
- Grep for "onnx", "ONNX", "export_to_onnx" in entire codebase: **0 matches**
- Only export formats supported are:
  - **GGUF** (llama.cpp format) 
  - **GGML** (LoRA GGML format)
  - **TorchAO** (PyTorch quantization)
  - **Hugging Face Hub** (Standard HF format with/without LoRA merging)

### 6. KEY ARCHITECTURAL INSIGHTS

#### Save Method Flow:

1. **LoRA-only save**:
   - Uses PEFT's default `save_pretrained()` 
   - Saves only A, B matrices and adapter config
   - Smallest file size (~5-10% of base model)

2. **Merged 16-bit save**:
   - Calls `_merge_lora()` on all LoRA layers
   - Dequantizes 4-bit to float32
   - Merges: `W_new = W_orig + A.T @ (s * B.T)`
   - Converts to float16
   - Compatible with llama.cpp/GGUF

3. **Merged 4-bit save**:
   - Uses `model.merge_and_unload()` from PEFT
   - Keeps quantization state
   - Useful for DPO/inference with lower VRAM

4. **GGUF export**:
   - Converts to merged 16-bit first
   - Installs llama.cpp
   - Converts HF format to GGUF via `convert-hf-to-gguf.py`
   - Quantizes via `llama-quantize` tool

#### Adapter Architecture:

- **Default adapter name**: `"default"`
- **Active adapters tracking**: `model.active_adapters` (list) or `model.active_adapter`
- **Multiple adapters**: Supported via PEFT (different adapters for different tasks)
- **Merging**: Can merge adapters back into base weights permanently

#### Weight Management:

- **Base layers**: `Bnb_Linear4bit` (bitsandbytes), `Peft_Linear4bit` (PEFT wrapped)
- **LoRA storage**: Separate `lora_A` and `lora_B` Linear layers
- **Quantization awareness**: Supports fake quantization (QAT) for training
- **Memory efficiency**: LoRA layers stay in low precision (usually int8 or fp16)

### 7. IMPORTANT FILE PATHS FOR REFERENCE

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/unsloth/save.py` | All save/export functionality (2679 lines) |
| `/unsloth/models/loader.py` | FastLanguageModel, FastModel classes |
| `/unsloth/models/llama.py` | Llama-specific optimizations & get_peft_model |
| `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` | LoRA forward/backward kernels |
| `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` | get_lora_parameters, get_lora_parameters_bias |
| `/unsloth/__init__.py` | Package initialization & public API |
| `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` | Save functionality tests |

This codebase is highly optimized for LoRA fine-tuning with support for 4-bit quantization, GGUF export, and efficient memory management on GPUs.

# Copyright 2023-present Daniel Han-Chen & the Unsloth team. All rights reserved.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.

import warnings, importlib, sys
from packaging.version import Version
import os, re, subprocess, inspect, functools
import numpy as np

# Fix some issues before importing other packages
from .import_fixes import fix_message_factory_issue
fix_message_factory_issue(); del fix_message_factory_issue;

# Check if modules that need patching are already imported
critical_modules = ['trl', 'transformers', 'peft']
already_imported = [mod for mod in critical_modules if mod in sys.modules]

# This check is critical because Unsloth optimizes these libraries by modifying
# their code at import time. If they're imported first, the original (slower,
# more memory-intensive) implementations will be used instead of Unsloth's
# optimized versions, potentially causing OOM errors or slower training.

if already_imported:
    # stacklevel=2 makes warning point to user's import line rather than this library code,
    # showing them exactly where to fix the import order in their script
    warnings.warn(
        f"WARNING: Unsloth should be imported before {', '.join(already_imported)} "
        f"to ensure all optimizations are applied. Your code may run slower or encounter "
        f"memory issues without these optimizations.\n\n"
        f"Please restructure your imports with 'import unsloth' at the top of your file.",
        stacklevel = 2,
    )
pass

# Unsloth currently does not work on multi GPU setups - sadly we are a 2 brother team so
# enabling it will require much more work, so we have to prioritize. Please understand!
# We do have a beta version, which you can contact us about!
# Thank you for your understanding and we appreciate it immensely!

# Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/1266
os.environ["PROTOCOL_BUFFERS_PYTHON_IMPLEMENTATION"] = "python"

# [TODO] Check why some GPUs don't work
#    "pinned_use_cuda_host_register:True,"\
#    "pinned_num_register_threads:8"

# Log Unsloth is being used
os.environ["UNSLOTH_IS_PRESENT"] = "1"

# Try importing PyTorch and check version
try:
    import torch
except ModuleNotFoundError:
    raise ImportError(
        "Unsloth: Pytorch is not installed. Go to https://pytorch.org/.\n"\
        "We have some installation instructions on our Github page."
    )
except Exception as exception:
    raise exception
pass

@functools.cache
def is_hip():
    return bool(getattr(getattr(torch, "version", None), "hip", None))
pass

@functools.cache
def get_device_type():
    if hasattr(torch, "cuda") and torch.cuda.is_available():
        if is_hip():
            return "hip"
        return "cuda"
    elif hasattr(torch, "xpu") and torch.xpu.is_available():
        return "xpu"
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth currently only works on NVIDIA GPUs and Intel GPUs.")
pass
DEVICE_TYPE : str = get_device_type()

@functools.cache
def get_device_count():
    if DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip"):
        return torch.cuda.device_count()
    elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
        return torch.xpu.device_count()
    else:
        return 1
pass

DEVICE_COUNT : int = get_device_count()

# Reduce VRAM usage by reducing fragmentation
# And optimize pinning of memory
# TODO(billishyahao): need to add hip related optimization...
if (DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip")) and (os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY", "0")=="0"):
    os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = \
        "expandable_segments:True,"\
        "roundup_power2_divisions:[32:256,64:128,256:64,>:32]"
elif (DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip")) and (os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY", "0")=="1") and \
    ("expandable_segments:True" in os.environ.get("PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF", "")):
    warnings.warn(
        "Unsloth: `UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY` is on, but requires `expandable_segments` to be off.\n"\
        "We will remove `expandable_segments`.",
        stacklevel = 2,
    )
    os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = re.sub(
        r"expandable\_segments\:True\,?",
        "",
        os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"],
    )
pass
# We support Pytorch 2
# Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/38
torch_version = str(re.match(r"[0-9\.]{3,}", str(torch.__version__)).group(0)).split(".")
major_torch, minor_torch = torch_version[0], torch_version[1]
major_torch, minor_torch = int(major_torch), int(minor_torch)
if (major_torch < 2):
    raise ImportError("Unsloth only supports Pytorch 2 for now. Please update your Pytorch to 2.1.\n"\
                      "We have some installation instructions on our Github page.")
elif (major_torch == 2) and (minor_torch < 2):
    # Disable expandable_segments
    del os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"]
pass

# CCE fails on Torch 2.8 and above
# OutOfResources: out of resource: shared memory, Required: 98304, Hardware limit: 65536. Reducing block sizes or `num_stages`
if (major_torch >= 2 and minor_torch >= 8) or (major_torch > 2):
    os.environ["UNSLOTH_ENABLE_CCE"] = "0"
pass

# Fix other issues
import importlib.util
from pathlib import Path
from importlib.metadata import version as importlib_version
from .import_fixes import fix_xformers_performance_issue
fix_xformers_performance_issue(); del fix_xformers_performance_issue;
from .import_fixes import fix_vllm_aimv2_issue
fix_vllm_aimv2_issue(); del fix_vllm_aimv2_issue;
from .import_fixes import ignore_logger_messages
ignore_logger_messages(); del ignore_logger_messages;

# Torch 2.4 has including_emulation
if DEVICE_TYPE == "cuda":
    major_version, minor_version = torch.cuda.get_device_capability()
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = (major_version >= 8)

    old_is_bf16_supported = torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported
    if "including_emulation" in str(inspect.signature(old_is_bf16_supported)):
        def is_bf16_supported(including_emulation = False):
            return old_is_bf16_supported(including_emulation)
        torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported = is_bf16_supported
    else:
        def is_bf16_supported(): return SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16
        torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported = is_bf16_supported
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "hip":
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported()
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
    # torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported() does not have including_emulation
    # set SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 as torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported()
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported()
pass

# For Gradio HF Spaces?
# if "SPACE_AUTHOR_NAME" not in os.environ and "SPACE_REPO_NAME" not in os.environ:
import triton
if DEVICE_TYPE == "cuda":
    libcuda_dirs = lambda: None
    if Version(triton.__version__) >= Version("3.0.0"):
        try: from triton.backends.nvidia.driver import libcuda_dirs
        except: pass
    else: from triton.common.build import libcuda_dirs

    # Try loading bitsandbytes and triton
    import bitsandbytes as bnb
    try:
        cdequantize_blockwise_fp32 = bnb.functional.lib.cdequantize_blockwise_fp32
        libcuda_dirs()
    except:
        warnings.warn(
            "Unsloth: Running `ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` to link CUDA."\
        )

        if os.path.exists("/usr/lib64-nvidia"):
            os.system("ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia")
        elif os.path.exists("/usr/local"):
            # Sometimes bitsandbytes cannot be linked properly in Runpod for example
            possible_cudas = subprocess.check_output(["ls", "-al", "/usr/local"]).decode("utf-8").split("\n")
            find_cuda = re.compile(r"[\s](cuda\-[\d\.]{2,})$")
            possible_cudas = [find_cuda.search(x) for x in possible_cudas]
            possible_cudas = [x.group(1) for x in possible_cudas if x is not None]

            # Try linking cuda folder, or everything in local
            if len(possible_cudas) == 0:
                os.system("ldconfig /usr/local/")
            else:
                find_number = re.compile(r"([\d\.]{2,})")
                latest_cuda = np.argsort([float(find_number.search(x).group(1)) for x in possible_cudas])[::-1][0]
                latest_cuda = possible_cudas[latest_cuda]
                os.system(f"ldconfig /usr/local/{latest_cuda}")
        pass

        importlib.reload(bnb)
        importlib.reload(triton)
        try:
            libcuda_dirs = lambda: None
            if Version(triton.__version__) >= Version("3.0.0"):
                try: from triton.backends.nvidia.driver import libcuda_dirs
                except: pass
            else: from triton.common.build import libcuda_dirs
            cdequantize_blockwise_fp32 = bnb.functional.lib.cdequantize_blockwise_fp32
            libcuda_dirs()
        except:
            warnings.warn(
                "Unsloth: CUDA is not linked properly.\n"\
                "Try running `python -m bitsandbytes` then `python -m xformers.info`\n"\
                "We tried running `ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` ourselves, but it didn't work.\n"\
                "You need to run in your terminal `sudo ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` yourself, then import Unsloth.\n"\
                "Also try `sudo ldconfig /usr/local/cuda-xx.x` - find the latest cuda version.\n"\
                "Unsloth will still run for now, but maybe it might crash - let's hope it works!"
            )
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "hip":
    # NO-OP for rocm device
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
    # currently intel xpu will not support bnb, will add support in the future
    # TODO: check triton for intel installed properly.
    pass

# Check for unsloth_zoo
try:
    unsloth_zoo_version = importlib_version("unsloth_zoo")
    if Version(unsloth_zoo_version) < Version("2025.9.6"):
        print(
            "Unsloth: Please update Unsloth and Unsloth-Zoo to the latest version!\n"\
            "Do this via `pip install --upgrade --force-reinstall --no-cache-dir --no-deps unsloth unsloth_zoo`"
        )
        # if os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_DISABLE_AUTO_UPDATES", "0") == "0":
        #     try:
        #         os.system("pip install --upgrade --no-cache-dir --no-deps unsloth_zoo")
        #     except:
        #         try:
        #             os.system("pip install --upgrade --no-cache-dir --no-deps --user unsloth_zoo")
        #         except:
        #             raise ImportError("Unsloth: Please update unsloth_zoo via `pip install --upgrade --no-cache-dir --no-deps unsloth_zoo`")
    import unsloth_zoo
except:
    raise ImportError("Unsloth: Please install unsloth_zoo via `pip install unsloth_zoo`")
pass

from .models import *
from .models import __version__
from .save import *
from .chat_templates import *
from .tokenizer_utils import *
from .trainer import *

# Patch TRL trainers for backwards compatibility
_patch_trl_trainer()

# Copyright 2023-present Daniel Han-Chen & the Unsloth team. All rights reserved.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at

# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.

from unsloth_zoo.utils import Version
from unsloth_zoo.hf_utils import dtype_from_config, HAS_TORCH_DTYPE
from bitsandbytes.nn import Linear4bit as Bnb_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear as Peft_Linear
from typing import Optional, Callable, Union, List
import sys
import requests
import torch
import os
import shutil
import pickle
import gc
from transformers.models.llama.modeling_llama import logger
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias
import subprocess
import psutil
import re
from transformers.models.llama.modeling_llama import logger
from .tokenizer_utils import fix_sentencepiece_gguf
from huggingface_hub import HfApi
try:
    from huggingface_hub import get_token
except:
    try:
        from huggingface_hub.utils import get_token
    except:
        # For older versions of huggingface_hub
        from huggingface_hub.utils._token import get_token
    pass
pass
from pathlib import Path

__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]

# llama.cpp specific targets - all takes 90s. Below takes 60s
LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS = ["llama-quantize", "llama-export-lora", "llama-cli",]

# Check environments
keynames = "\n" + "\n".join(os.environ.keys())
IS_COLAB_ENVIRONMENT  = "\nCOLAB_"  in keynames
IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT = "\nKAGGLE_" in keynames
KAGGLE_TMP = "/tmp"
del keynames

# Weights
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)
LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
    "input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm",
)

# https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/master/examples/quantize/quantize.cpp#L19
# From https://mlabonne.github.io/blog/posts/Quantize_Llama_2_models_using_ggml.html
ALLOWED_QUANTS = \
{
    "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
    "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",

gc.collect()

def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

torch_float16  = torch.float16
torch_bfloat16 = torch.bfloat16

def QUANT_STATE(W): return getattr(W, "quant_state", None)

def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
pass


def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass


def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.

def unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged(
    self,
    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    token                : Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.push_to_hub(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]          = self
    arguments["save_directory"] = repo_id
    arguments["push_to_hub"]    = True
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["repo_id"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    torchao_config       = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
):
    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint

    Args
      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
    """
    # first merge the lora weights
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["torchao_config"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
    from torchao import quantize_
    if torchao_config is None:
        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)

    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
    else:
        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
        arguments["save_directory"],
        device_map = "auto",
        quantization_config = quantization_config,
        **kwargs,
    )

    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"

    if push_to_hub:
        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
    else:
        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
    pass
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass

@torch.inference_mode
def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

    # Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None: token = get_token()

    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()

    if commit_description is None:
        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
    pass

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
        )
    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
        save_method = "merged_4bit"
    pass

    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
    pass

    # First check for a token!
    if push_to_hub:
        from huggingface_hub import whoami
        try:
            username = whoami(token = token)["name"]
        except:
            raise RuntimeError(
                "Unsloth: Please supply a token!\n"\
                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens"
            )
        pass
    pass

    assert(maximum_memory_usage > 0 and maximum_memory_usage <= 0.95)

    # Clean memory up first
    for _ in range(3):
        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
        gc.collect()
    pass

    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")
    if save_method != "lora" and save_method != "merged_16bit" and save_method != "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: You must select one of 3 options when saving models:\n"\
            '"lora"         ==> This is the fastest and easiet. Just saves LoRA modules.\n'\
            '"merged_16bit" ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to float16. Needed for llama.cpp / GGUF.\n'\
            '"merged_4bit"  ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to 4bit. Useful for DPO / inference.'
        )
    pass

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":

        print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 4bit...")
        print("This might take 5 minutes...")

        # Counteract no LoRA adapters!
        if hasattr(model, "merge_and_unload"):
            model = model.merge_and_unload()
        pass
        print("Done.")
    pass

    if tags is not None:
        assert(isinstance(tags, (list, tuple)))
        tags = list(tags) + ["unsloth",]
    else:
        tags = ["unsloth",]
    pass
    save_pretrained_settings["tags"] = tags

    if ((save_method == "lora") or (save_method == "merged_4bit")) and push_to_hub:
        if token is None:
            raise RuntimeError(
                "Unsloth: Pushing to HF requires a token. Pass `token = 'hf_....'`\n"\
                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens."
            )
        pass
